channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: White House to host first Crypto summit
item.description: The White House is set to welcome professionals in the cryptocurrency industry on March 7 for a first-of-its-kind summit.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office
item.description: Thomas Shedd, the commissioner of Federal Acquisition Service, said the decision is part of the President’s Workforce Optimization Initiative executive order. The post After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Curious about DeepSeek but worried about privacy? These apps let you use an LLM without the internet
item.description: Most of us are used to using internet chatbots like ChatGPT and DeepSeek in one of two ways: via a web browser or via their dedicated smartphone apps. There are two drawbacks to this. First, their use requires an internet connection.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: After rolling out Sora beyond the US, OpenAI plans to put the video AI tool inside ChatGPT
item.description: ChatGPT can already generate text and images, and it seems video will be added to the list eventually.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Investment research data breach exposes 12 million customers
item.description: A recent ransomeware attack targeted Zacks, an American investment firm, and a cybercriminal claimed to have stolen records for millions of customers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Brand new Alexa
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: This smart baby monitor with dual mode and enhanced alerts from Momcozy will give new parents peace of mind
item.description: The Momcozy 5-inch Dual-Mode Smart Baby Monitor is a great choice for parents who want to keep an eye on their little ones. It's packed with features including motion and cry detection and temperature monitoring, so you can be certain that your baby is in good hands when monitored by Momcozy.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: DOGE isn’t Silicon Valley innovation—it’s just a sloppy rebrand of free-market dogma
item.description: At a press conference in the Oval Office earlier this month, Elon Musk—a billionaire who is not, at least formally, the President of the United States—was asked how the Department of Government Efficiency manages potential conflicts of interest to ensure “accountability and transparency.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: TrapC proposal to fix C/C++ memory safety
item.description: TrapC, a fork of the C language, is being developed as a potential solution for memory safety issues that have hindered the C and C++ languages. TrapC is a proposed C-language extension compiler intended to be implemented as a cybersecurity compiler for C and C++ code, said developer Robin Rowe.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Energy Department scientists participate in 'AI Jam' with OpenAI, Anthropic
item.description: Nearly 1,000 scientists from multiple U.S. National Laboratories gathered Friday to test artificial intelligence (AI) models from leading firms like OpenAI and Anthropic in an effort to harness the advancing technology for science and national security purposes.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Tech layoffs this year: A timeline
item.description: 2025 began in turmoil, with layoffs at some of the largest tech companies despite the support shown by the new US administration. 2024 had been a year of recovery, with the pace of layoffs slowing and IT employment the highest for years following two years of massive IT layoff in 2022 and 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: R.I.P. Skype: 2003-2025
item.description: The time is up for Skype, one of the O.G.s of online communication. The once-popular 22-year-old communications software is being retired by Microsoft in May 2025, with the company’s Teams collaboration suite replacing its predecessor.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011 and which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Apple unveils 'age assurance' technology amid child safety push
item.description: Apple will introduce "age assurance" technology to boost children's privacy and safety on the company's devices, the company announced Thursday as part of its broader push to protect kids online.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Tens of Thousands of Misconfigured AMS Exposed Globally - A Major Cybersecurity Problem
item.description: Researchers find 49,000 misconfigured Access Management Systems (AMS) worldwide, exposing organizations to cyberattacks and data breaches.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to get Sharp Fang in Monster Hunter Wilds
item.description: Here's where to find Sharp Fang in Monster Hunter Wilds, so that you can start crafting better armor sets and weapons.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Warren presses X on deleted Trump nominee’s posts
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) is pressing the social platform X about one of President Trump’s economic nominees seemingly scraping many posts from his account. In a letter addressed to Linda Yaccarino, X's CEO, Warren asked the company to recover more than 24,000 posts from William Pulte.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Scientists scorn EPA push to say climate change isn't a danger, say just look around at the world
item.description: As President Donald Trump's administration looks to reverse a cornerstone finding that climate change endangers human health and welfare, scientists say they need to look around because it’s obvious how bad global warming is and how its getting worse.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meme coins aren’t just harmless fun
item.description: For some time, meme coins have occupied a peculiar space in online culture. While there are people who have struck it rich trading these joke-based cryptocurrencies, the landscape is riddled with scams, “rug pulls,” and market manipulation.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: IBM closes Hashicorp buy, looks to boost enterprise multicloud and AI automation technology
item.description: In announcing the closure of its $6.4 billion buy of Hashicorp, IBM said its goal is to infuse HashiCorp automation and security technology in every data center possible.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bipartisan senators unveil bill banning DeepSeek on government devices
item.description: A bipartisan group of senators introduced legislation Thursday to ban Chinese artificial intelligence (AI) startup DeepSeek’s products from government devices and networks. The bill, introduced by Sens. Jacky Rosen (D-Nev.), Jon Husted (R-Ohio) and Pete Ricketts (R-Neb.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: NFL NextGen Stats team unveils new tool to help fans better understand combine results
item.description: The NFL scouting combine provides a blizzard of data with hundreds of prospects running through various drills ranging from the 40-yard dash to the vertical jump to a short shuttle.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kandji exec: Vision Pro is a good fit for the enterprise
item.description: Apple’s recently announced partnership with Dassault Systemés shows the Vision Pro as the cutting-edge enterprise solution it has become, but business users will want to ensure these devices can be managed like the rest of their critical IT infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Signal will exit Sweden rather than dilute message security
item.description: The CEO of Signal said Tuesday that the service will leave Sweden rather than comply with a rule that will require vendors to capture all secure messages and save a plain text copy, in case authorities later want to subpoena that data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Get started with async in Python
item.description: Asynchronous programming, or async, is a feature of many modern languages that allows a program to juggle multiple operations without waiting or getting hung up on any one of them.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI, automation spur efforts to upskill network pros
item.description: Networking skills are evolving to keep pace with broader technology trends, and network pros need to proactively develop new competencies in areas such as AI and automation to stay relevant.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Atlassian refocuses Trello on individual task management
item.description: Atlassian’s Trello project management tool has received a facelift with features designed to organize meetings and ideas in a cohesive manner.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IBM targets AI application growth with DataStax buy
item.description: Looking to bolster its AI data handling and storage capabilities, IBM said it had entered an agreement to buy open-source database developer DataStax for an undisclosed amount.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Connolly calls on OPM to reverse CIO classification change
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly, the ranking member of the Oversight and Accountability Committee, said the CIOs should remain career-reserve SES positions. The post Connolly calls on OPM to reverse CIO classification change first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How VR technology is curing loneliness in seniors
item.description: VR transforms senior care: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says immersive tech helps older adults explore and connect via virtual experiences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protecting US supply chains from foreign influence
item.description: With the new Trump administration likely to place an even greater focus on reducing reliance on China, this trend is expected to accelerate. The post Protecting US supply chains from foreign influence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Elizabeth Holmes, the disgraced founder of Theranos, will remain in prison after losing a bid Monday to overturn her fraud conviction, with a federal appeals court saying she hadn’t proved there were legal missteps during her trial for defrauding investors with false claims of w
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
